title: TC-FW-LANG-001: Language selection UI stale after firmware update — verify app restart required to reflect new language
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone (e.g., Poly Sync 20).
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
3. Note the currently selected language (e.g., 'English').
4. Go to Device → Software & Updates and check for a firmware update.
5. If an update is available, tap the update banner and confirm the update.
6. Wait for the firmware update to complete (device restarts automatically).
7. After device reconnects, immediately navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection WITHOUT closing the app.
8. Observe the language dropdown value displayed.
9. Change the language selection to a different language (e.g., 'Deutsch').
10. Tap 'Apply' or 'Save' if prompted.
11. Observe the result of the language change attempt.
expected: Step 8: The language dropdown still shows the pre-update language (e.g., 'English'), not reflecting any language changes from the firmware update. Step 11: The app displays the 'Update currently not available' dialog or the language change is silently rejected. The UI state is stale and does not match the actual device state after the firmware update.

title: TC-FW-LANG-002: Language selection works correctly only after app restart post-firmware-update
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection and note the current language.
3. Go to Device → Software & Updates and perform a firmware update.
4. Wait for the update to complete and the device to restart and reconnect.
5. Without changing any settings, fully close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents or force-stop).
6. Relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the device to reconnect.
7. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
8. Observe the language dropdown value.
9. Change the language to a different option (e.g., from 'English' to 'Français').
10. Tap 'Apply' or 'Save'.
11. Navigate away from Audio Settings (e.g., tap back to Device screen), then return to Audio Settings → Language Selection.
12. Verify the language selection persists.
expected: Step 8: The language dropdown now reflects the correct post-update language state (may differ from pre-update if the firmware changed language options). Step 10: The language change is accepted without any error dialog. Step 12: The selected language persists after navigating away and back.

title: TC-FW-LANG-003: 'Update currently not available' dialog appears when changing language post-firmware-update without app restart
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection and set language to 'English'.
3. Go to Device → Software & Updates and trigger a firmware update.
4. Wait for the firmware update to complete and the device to reconnect.
5. Do NOT restart the app. Immediately navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
6. Tap the language dropdown and select a different language (e.g., 'Español').
7. Tap 'Apply' or 'Save'.
8. Observe the dialog or message that appears.
9. Dismiss the dialog, then fully close and restart the Poly Studio Mobile app.
10. Navigate again to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
11. Attempt to change the language again to 'Español' and tap 'Apply'.
expected: Step 8: The app displays the 'Update currently not available' dialog, preventing the language change. Step 11: After app restart, the language change succeeds without any error dialog, confirming the issue was a stale UI state.

title: TC-FW-LANG-004: Firmware version display stale after firmware update — requires app restart to refresh
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates.
3. Note the current firmware version displayed (e.g., '1.2.3').
4. Perform a firmware update to a newer version (e.g., '1.2.4').
5. Wait for the update to complete and the device to restart and reconnect.
6. Immediately navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates WITHOUT restarting the app.
7. Observe the firmware version displayed.
8. Fully close and restart the Poly Studio Mobile app.
9. Navigate again to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates.
10. Observe the firmware version displayed.
expected: Step 7: The firmware version still shows the old version (e.g., '1.2.3') despite the update completing successfully. Step 10: After app restart, the firmware version correctly shows the new version (e.g., '1.2.4'). This confirms the firmware version display is not refreshed after DFU without an app restart.

title: TC-FW-LANG-005: Language selection edge case — rapid language switching after firmware update without app restart
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
3. Set language to 'English'.
4. Go to Device → Software & Updates and perform a firmware update.
5. Wait for the update to complete and the device to reconnect.
6. Without restarting the app, navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
7. Rapidly switch the language dropdown between 3 different languages (e.g., 'Deutsch' → 'Français' → 'Español'), tapping 'Apply' after each change.
8. Observe the behavior after each attempt.
9. Close and restart the Poly Studio Mobile app.
10. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
11. Check which language is currently selected.
12. Attempt to change to a different language and verify it works.
expected: Step 8: Each language change attempt shows the 'Update currently not available' dialog or is silently rejected. The dropdown may reset to the pre-update language after each failed attempt. Step 11: After app restart, the language shown is the pre-update language (since no changes were persisted). Step 12: Language change succeeds after restart, confirming the stale UI state was the root cause.

title: TC-FW-LANG-006: Language selection UI state after firmware update that adds new language options
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone running firmware version A (which supports languages: English, Deutsch, Français).
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection and verify the available languages match the expected list.
3. Go to Device → Software & Updates and update firmware to version B (which adds support for 'Italiano' and 'Español').
4. Wait for the update to complete and the device to reconnect.
5. Without restarting the app, navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
6. Tap the language dropdown and observe the list of available languages.
7. Check if 'Italiano' and 'Español' appear in the list.
8. Fully close and restart the Poly Studio Mobile app.
9. Navigate again to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
10. Tap the language dropdown and observe the list of available languages.
expected: Step 6-7: The language dropdown still shows only the old language list (English, Deutsch, Français). The new languages ('Italiano', 'Español') are NOT visible. Step 10: After app restart, the dropdown correctly shows the updated language list including 'Italiano' and 'Español'. This confirms that language capability changes from firmware updates are not reflected in the UI until the app is restarted.

title: TC-FW-LANG-007: App lifecycle edge case — app backgrounded during firmware update, language state on return
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection and set to 'English'.
3. Go to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
4. While the update is in progress (e.g., at 40-60%), press the Home button to background the app.
5. Wait 2 minutes, then reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher.
6. Wait for the firmware update to complete (device restarts and reconnects).
7. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
8. Attempt to change the language to 'Deutsch' and tap 'Apply'.
9. Observe the result.
10. Fully close and restart the app, then repeat step 7-8.
expected: Step 8: The 'Update currently not available' dialog appears or the change is rejected. Step 10: After app restart, the language change succeeds. The app's UI state was not refreshed even after being backgrounded and brought back to the foreground during the update lifecycle.

title: TC-FW-LANG-008: Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect post-firmware-update — language UI refresh check
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a supported Bluetooth speakerphone.
2. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection and set to 'English'.
3. Go to Device → Software & Updates and perform a firmware update.
4. Wait for the update to complete and the device to restart and reconnect.
5. Without restarting the app, manually disconnect the Bluetooth connection from the phone's Bluetooth settings.
6. Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect to the device from the phone's Bluetooth settings.
7. Wait for the Poly Studio Mobile app to detect and reconnect to the device.
8. Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language Selection.
9. Observe the language dropdown value.
10. Attempt to change the language and tap 'Apply'.
11. Observe the result.
expected: Step 9: The language dropdown still shows the pre-update language ('English') — a Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect does NOT refresh the stale UI state. Step 10-11: The language change attempt shows the 'Update currently not available' dialog or is rejected. The UI state remains stale despite the Bluetooth reconnection.
